disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chalk-white macules with completely normal skin texture — no scale/raised border/central clearing (against tinea/pityriasis versicolor), no ulceration/necrosis, no chemical exposure
trigger_question_examples: Is there any flaky scale, a raised red active border, or blistering/open skin on the patches?
Any strong chemical or bleach exposure to the area?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No scale, no raised border, no blisters or open skin, and no chemical/bleach exposure — the skin feels smooth and normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Painless, non-itchy, afebrile; the surrounding erythema is self-induced by scrubbing and blanches
trigger_question_examples: Are the patches painful, itchy, or is there a fever?
Is the red rim from your scrubbing, and does it fade when pressed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No pain, no itch, no fever; the red rim is where I scrubbed and it fades when I press it.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a contagious fungus or parasite eating my skin?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that chalk-white patches with completely normal, smooth, painless skin and no scale are benign loss of skin pigment (loss of pigment), not a fungus/parasite/burn, and you can't wash it away; advise stopping the scrubbing, gentle care and daily sunscreen to the depigmented skin, with an infection/necrosis safety-net — without ED over-triage.
